QF.id: VIII Sales Practices and Investor Protection Services_8

question.text: The arbitration mechanism at stock exchanges for derivative disputes:

answer.text: Provides a cost-effective and time-bound alternative to court proceedings for resolving disputes between investors and brokers
Is available only for disputes exceeding Rs. 50 lakhs
Requires both parties to agree before the dispute can be heard
Is a voluntary mechanism with no binding outcome

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Exchange arbitration provides a quick, cost-effective dispute resolution mechanism. Key features: (1) Time-bound process (typically 4 months), (2) Lower cost than court proceedings, (3) Panel of qualified arbitrators, (4) Binding outcome (can be appealed), (5) Available for disputes of varying sizes. The arbitration clause is part of the member-client agreement.
**The Trap:** Arbitration is available for disputes of all sizes, not just large ones. The outcome IS binding (not voluntary). It is included in the standard member-client agreement.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Dispute resolution mechanisms are part of investor protection infrastructure.
**Key Takeaway:** Exchange arbitration: time-bound, cost-effective, binding, available for all dispute sizes. Part of member-client agreement.
